title: Vibrant Sour Peruvian Cream Flatbread
description: A chewy flatbread with a bright citrus tang and creamy finish, perfect for kids and adults alike.
instructions: In a large bowl stir the flour, salt, sugar, cumin and potato starch until well blended.
Add the warm water and olive oil to the dry mix and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Fold in the single cream and lime juice, then knead the dough for about 3 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with a damp towel and let it rest for 5 minutes to relax the gluten.
Divide the rested dough into four equal portions and roll each into a thin circle about 10 cm in diameter.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and cook each flatbread for 2 minutes on each side or until lightly golden.
While the flatbread cooks, sprinkle chopped cilantro over the top of each piece.
Serve the flatbread warm, optionally with a side of fresh salsa or a light yogurt dip.
If not serving immediately, wrap the flatbread in foil to keep it soft.
Enjoy the sour, creamy, kid-friendly Peruvian flatbread as part of a hearty lunch.
